name: bincopy

metadata.name: bincopy
metadata.summary: Mangling of various file formats that conveys binary information (Motorola S-Record, Intel HEX and binary files).

documentation:
# About

Mangling of various file formats that conveys binary information
(Motorola S-Record, Intel HEX, TI-TXT, Verilog VMEM, ELF and binary
files).

Project homepage: <https://github.com/eerimoq/bincopy>

Documentation: <https://bincopy.readthedocs.io>

# Installation

```python
pip install bincopy
```

# Example usage

## Scripting

A basic example converting from Intel HEX to Intel HEX, SREC, binary,
array and hexdump formats:

```pycon
>>> import bincopy
>>> f = bincopy.BinFile("tests/files/in.hex")
>>> print(f.as_ihex())
:20010000214601360121470136007EFE09D219012146017E17C20001FF5F16002148011979
:20012000194E79234623965778239EDA3F01B2CA3F0156702B5E712B722B7321460134219F
:00000001FF

>>> print(f.as_srec())
S32500000100214601360121470136007EFE09D219012146017E17C20001FF5F16002148011973
S32500000120194E79234623965778239EDA3F01B2CA3F0156702B5E712B722B73214601342199
S5030002FA

>>> print(f.as_ti_txt())
@0100
21 46 01 36 01 21 47 01 36 00 7E FE 09 D2 19 01
21 46 01 7E 17 C2 00 01 FF 5F 16 00 21 48 01 19
19 4E 79 23 46 23 96 57 78 23 9E DA 3F 01 B2 CA
3F 01 56 70 2B 5E 71 2B 72 2B 73 21 46 01 34 21
q

>>> print(f.as_verilog_vmem())
@00000100 21 46 01 36 01 21 47 01 36 00 7E FE 09 D2 19 01 21 46 01 7E 17 C2 00 01 FF 5F 16 00 21 48 01 19
@00000120 19 4E 79 23 46 23 96 57 78 23 9E DA 3F 01 B2 CA 3F 01 56 70 2B 5E 71 2B 72 2B 73 21 46 01 34 21

>>> f.as_binary()
bytearray(b'!F\x016\x01!G\x016\x00~\xfe\t\xd2\x19\x01!F\x01~\x17\xc2\x00\x01
\xff_\x16\x00!H\x01\x19\x19Ny#F#\x96Wx#\x9e\xda?\x01\xb2\xca?\x01Vp+^q+r+s!
F\x014!')
>>> list(f.segments)
[Segment(address=256, data=bytearray(b'!F\x016\x01!G\x016\x00~\xfe\t\xd2\x19\x01
!F\x01~\x17\xc2\x00\x01\xff_\x16\x00!H\x01\x19\x19Ny#F#\x96Wx#\x9e\xda?\x01
\xb2\xca?\x01Vp+^q+r+s!F\x014!'))]
>>> f.minimum_address
256
>>> f.maximum_address
320
>>> len(f)
64
>>> f[f.minimum_address]
33
>>> f[f.minimum_address:f.minimum_address + 1]
bytearray(b'!')
```

See the [test suite] for additional examples.

## Command line tool

### The info subcommand

Print general information about given binary format file(s).

```text
$ bincopy info tests/files/in.hex
File:                    tests/files/in.hex
Data ranges:

    0x00000100 - 0x00000140 (64 bytes)

Data ratio:              100.0 %
Layout:

    0x100                                                      0x140
    ================================================================
```

### The convert subcommand

Convert file(s) from one format to another.

```text
$ bincopy convert -i ihex -o srec tests/files/in.hex -
S32500000100214601360121470136007EFE09D219012146017E17C20001FF5F16002148011973
S32500000120194E79234623965778239EDA3F01B2CA3F0156702B5E712B722B73214601342199
S5030002FA
$ bincopy convert -i binary -o hexdump tests/files/in.hex -
00000000  3a 32 30 30 31 30 30 30  30 32 31 34 36 30 31 33  |:200100002146013|
00000010  36 30 31 32 31 34 37 30  31 33 36 30 30 37 45 46  |60121470136007EF|
00000020  45 30 39 44 32 31 39 30  31 32 31 34 36 30 31 37  |E09D219012146017|
00000030  45 31 37 43 32 30 30 30  31 46 46 35 46 31 36 30  |E17C20001FF5F160|
00000040  30 32 31 34 38 30 31 31  39 37 39 0a 3a 32 30 30  |02148011979.:200|
00000050  31 32 30 30 30 31 39 34  45 37 39 32 33 34 36 32  |12000194E7923462|
00000060  33 39 36 35 37 37 38 32  33 39 45 44 41 33 46 30  |3965778239EDA3F0|
00000070  31 42 32 43 41 33 46 30  31 35 36 37 30 32 42 35  |1B2CA3F0156702B5|
00000080  45 37 31 32 42 37 32 32  42 37 33 32 31 34 36 30  |E712B722B7321460|
00000090  31 33 34 32 31 39 46 0a  3a 30 30 30 30 30 30 30  |134219F.:0000000|
000000a0  31 46 46 0a                                       |1FF.            |
```

Concatenate two or more files.

```text
$ bincopy convert -o srec tests/files/in.s19 tests/files/convert.s19 -
S00F000068656C6C6F202020202000003C
S325000000007C0802A6900100049421FFF07C6C1B787C8C23783C600000386300004BFFFFE5F2
S32500000020398000007D83637880010014382100107C0803A64E80002048656C6C6F20776F13
S30B00000040726C642E0A003A
S32500000100214601360121470136007EFE09D219012146017E17C20001FF5F16002148011973
S32500000120194E79234623965778239EDA3F01B2CA3F0156702B5E712B722B73214601342199
S5030005F7
S70500000000FA
```

### The pretty subcommand

Easy to read Motorola S-Record, Intel HEX and TI TXT files with the
pretty subcommand.

```{image} https://github.com/eerimoq/bincopy/raw/master/docs/pretty-s19.png
```

```{image} https://github.com/eerimoq/bincopy/raw/master/docs/pretty-hex.png
```

```{image} https://github.com/eerimoq/bincopy/raw/master/docs/pretty-ti-txt.png
```

### The fill subcommand

Fill empty space between segments. Use `--max-words` to only fill
gaps smaller than given size.

```text
$ bincopy info tests/files/in_exclude_2_4.s19 | grep byte
    0x00000000 - 0x00000002 (2 bytes)
    0x00000004 - 0x00000046 (66 bytes)
$ bincopy fill tests/files/in_exclude_2_4.s19 filled.s19
$ bincopy info filled.s19 | grep byte
    0x00000000 - 0x00000046 (70 bytes)
```

# Contributing

1. Fork the repository.

2. Install prerequisites.

   ```text
   pip install -r requirements.txt
   ```

3. Implement the new feature or bug fix.

4. Implement test case(s) to ensure that future changes do not break
   legacy.

5. Run the tests.

   ```text
   make test
   ```

6. Create a pull request.

# Similar projects

These projects provides features similar to bincopy:

- [SRecord] (`srec_cat` and `srec_info`)
- [IntelHex] (Python IntelHex library)
- [objutils] (Process HEX files in Python)

[intelhex]: https://github.com/python-intelhex/intelhex
[objutils]: https://github.com/christoph2/objutils
[srecord]: http://srecord.sourceforge.net/
[test suite]: https://github.com/eerimoq/bincopy/blob/master/tests/test_bincopy.py

modules.name: bincopy
documentation:
Mangling of various file formats that conveys binary information
(Motorola S-Record, Intel HEX, TI-TXT and binary files).

functions.name: bincopy.crc_srec
documentation: Calculate the CRC for given Motorola S-Record hexstring.

functions.name: bincopy.crc_ihex
documentation: Calculate the CRC for given Intel HEX hexstring.

functions.name: bincopy.pack_srec
documentation: Create a Motorola S-Record record of given data.

functions.name: bincopy.unpack_srec
documentation: Unpack given Motorola S-Record record into variables.

functions.name: bincopy.pack_ihex
documentation: Create a Intel HEX record of given data.

functions.name: bincopy.unpack_ihex
documentation: Unpack given Intel HEX record into variables.

functions.name: bincopy.pretty_srec
documentation: Make given Motorola S-Record pretty by adding colors to it.

functions.name: bincopy.pretty_ihex
documentation: Make given Intel HEX record pretty by adding colors to it.

functions.name: bincopy.pretty_ti_txt
documentation: Make given TI TXT line pretty by adding colors to it.

classes.name: bincopy.Error
documentation: Bincopy base exception.

classes.name: bincopy.Segment

methods.name: bincopy.Segment.chunks
documentation:
Yield data chunks of `size` words, aligned as given by `alignment`.

Each chunk is itself a Segment.

`size` and `alignment` are in words. `size` must be a multiple of
`alignment`. If set, `padding` must be a word value.

If `padding` is set, the first and final chunks are padded so that:
    1. The first chunk is aligned even if the segment itself is not.
    2. The final chunk's size is a multiple of `alignment`.

methods.name: bincopy.Segment.add_data
documentation:
Add given data to this segment. The added data must be adjacent to
the current segment data, otherwise an exception is thrown.

methods.name: bincopy.Segment.remove_data
documentation:
Remove given data range from this segment. Returns the second
segment if the removed data splits this segment in two.
A segment is a chunk data with given minimum and maximum address.

classes.name: bincopy.Segments

methods.name: bincopy.Segments.__iter__
documentation: Iterate over all segments.

methods.name: bincopy.Segments.minimum_address
documentation:
The minimum address of the data, or `None` if no data is
available.

methods.name: bincopy.Segments.maximum_address
documentation:
The maximum address of the data, or `None` if no data is
available.

methods.name: bincopy.Segments.add
documentation: Add segments by ascending address.

methods.name: bincopy.Segments.chunks
documentation:
Iterate over all segments and yield chunks of the data.

The chunks are `size` words long, aligned as given by `alignment`.

Each chunk is itself a Segment.

`size` and `alignment` are in words. `size` must be a multiple of
`alignment`. If set, `padding` must be a word value.

If `padding` is set, the first and final chunks of each segment are
padded so that:
    1. The first chunk is aligned even if the segment itself is not.
    2. The final chunk's size is a multiple of `alignment`.

methods.name: bincopy.Segments.__len__
documentation: Get the number of segments.
A list of segments.

classes.name: bincopy.BinFile

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.__setitem__
documentation: Write data to given absolute address or address range.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.__getitem__
documentation: Read data from given absolute address or address range.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.__len__
documentation: Number of words in the file.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.execution_start_address
documentation: The execution start address, or `None` if missing.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.minimum_address
documentation: The minimum address of the data, or `None` if the file is empty.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.maximum_address
documentation:
The maximum address of the data plus one, or `None` if the file
is empty.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.header
documentation:
The binary file header, or `None` if missing. See
{class}`BinFile's<.BinFile>` `header_encoding` argument for
encoding options.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.segments
documentation:
The segments object. Can be used to iterate over all segments in
the binary.

Below is an example iterating over all segments, two in this
case, and printing them.

```pycon
>>> for segment in binfile.segments:
...     print(segment)
...
Segment(address=0, data=bytearray(b'\x00\x01\x02'))
Segment(address=10, data=bytearray(b'\x03\x04\x05'))
```

All segments can be split into smaller pieces using the
`chunks(size=32, alignment=1)` method.

```pycon
>>> for chunk in binfile.segments.chunks(2):
...     print(chunk)
...
Segment(address=0, data=bytearray(b'\x00\x01'))
Segment(address=2, data=bytearray(b'\x02'))
Segment(address=10, data=bytearray(b'\x03\x04'))
Segment(address=12, data=bytearray(b'\x05'))
```

Each segment can be split into smaller pieces using the
`chunks(size=32, alignment=1)` method on a single segment.

```pycon
>>> for segment in binfile.segments:
...     print(segment)
...     for chunk in segment.chunks(2):
...         print(chunk)
...
Segment(address=0, data=bytearray(b'\x00\x01\x02'))
Segment(address=0, data=bytearray(b'\x00\x01'))
Segment(address=2, data=bytearray(b'\x02'))
Segment(address=10, data=bytearray(b'\x03\x04\x05'))
Segment(address=10, data=bytearray(b'\x03\x04'))
Segment(address=12, data=bytearray(b'\x05'))
```

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add
documentation:
Add given data string by guessing its format. The format must be
Motorola S-Records, Intel HEX or TI-TXT. Set `overwrite` to
`True` to allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_srec
documentation:
Add given Motorola S-Records string. Set `overwrite` to `True` to
allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_ihex
documentation:
Add given Intel HEX records string. Set `overwrite` to `True` to
allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_ti_txt
documentation:
Add given TI-TXT string `lines`. Set `overwrite` to `True` to
allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_binary
documentation:
Add given data at given address. Set `overwrite` to `True` to
allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_elf
documentation: Add given ELF data.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_microchip_hex
documentation:
Add given Microchip HEX data.

Microchip's HEX format is identical to Intel's except an address in
the HEX file is twice the actual machine address. For example:

:02000E00E4C943

    :       Start code
    02      Record contains two data bytes
    000E    Address 0x000E; Machine address is 0x000E // 2 == 0x0007
    00      Record type is data
    E4      Low byte at address 0x0007 is 0xE4
    C9      High byte at address 0x0007 is 0xC9

Microchip HEX records therefore need to be parsed as if the word size
is one byte, but the parsed data must be handled as if the word size
is two bytes. This is true for both 8-bit PICs such as PIC18 and
16-bit PICs such as PIC24.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_file
documentation:
Open given file and add its data by guessing its format. The format
must be Motorola S-Records, Intel HEX, TI-TXT. Set `overwrite`
to `True` to allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_srec_file
documentation:
Open given Motorola S-Records file and add its records. Set
`overwrite` to `True` to allow already added data to be
overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_ihex_file
documentation:
Open given Intel HEX file and add its records. Set `overwrite` to
`True` to allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_ti_txt_file
documentation:
Open given TI-TXT file and add its contents. Set `overwrite` to
`True` to allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_verilog_vmem_file
documentation:
Open given Verilog VMEM file and add its contents. Set `overwrite` to
`True` to allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_binary_file
documentation:
Open given binary file and add its contents. Set `overwrite` to
`True` to allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_elf_file
documentation:
Open given ELF file and add its contents. Set `overwrite` to
`True` to allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.add_microchip_hex_file
documentation:
Open given Microchip HEX file and add its contents. Set `overwrite`
to `True` to allow already added data to be overwritten.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.as_srec
documentation:
Format the binary file as Motorola S-Records records and return
them as a string.

`number_of_data_bytes` is the number of data bytes in each
record.

`address_length_bits` is the number of address bits in each
record.

```pycon
>>> print(binfile.as_srec())
S32500000100214601360121470136007EFE09D219012146017E17C20001FF5F16002148011973
S32500000120194E79234623965778239EDA3F01B2CA3F0156702B5E712B722B73214601342199
S5030002FA
```

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.as_ihex
documentation:
Format the binary file as Intel HEX records and return them as a
string.

`number_of_data_bytes` is the number of data bytes in each
record.

`address_length_bits` is the number of address bits in each
record.

```pycon
>>> print(binfile.as_ihex())
:20010000214601360121470136007EFE09D219012146017E17C20001FF5F16002148011979
:20012000194E79234623965778239EDA3F01B2CA3F0156702B5E712B722B7321460134219F
:00000001FF
```

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.as_ti_txt
documentation:
Format the binary file as a TI-TXT file and return it as a string.

```pycon
>>> print(binfile.as_ti_txt())
@0100
21 46 01 36 01 21 47 01 36 00 7E FE 09 D2 19 01
21 46 01 7E 17 C2 00 01 FF 5F 16 00 21 48 01 19
19 4E 79 23 46 23 96 57 78 23 9E DA 3F 01 B2 CA
3F 01 56 70 2B 5E 71 2B 72 2B 73 21 46 01 34 21
q
```

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.as_verilog_vmem
documentation:
Format the binary file as a Verilog VMEM file and return it as a string.

```pycon
>>> print(binfile.as_verilog_vmem())
```

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.as_binary
documentation:
Return a byte string of all data within given address range.

`minimum_address` is the absolute minimum address of the
resulting binary data (including). By default this is the
minimum address in the binary.

`maximum_address` is the absolute maximum address of the
resulting binary data (excluding). By default this is the
maximum address in the binary plus one.

`padding` is the word value of the padding between
non-adjacent segments. Give as a bytes object of length 1 when
the word size is 8 bits, length 2 when the word size is 16
bits, and so on. By default the padding is `b'\xff' *
word_size_bytes`.

```pycon
>>> binfile.as_binary()
bytearray(b'!F\x016\x01!G\x016\x00~\xfe\t\xd2\x19\x01!F\x01~\x17\xc2\x00\x01
\xff_\x16\x00!H\x01\x19\x19Ny#F#\x96Wx#\x9e\xda?\x01\xb2\xca?\x01Vp+^q+r+s!
F\x014!')
```

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.as_array
documentation:
Format the binary file as a string values separated by given
separator `separator`. This function can be used to generate
array initialization code for C and other languages.

`minimum_address` is the absolute minimum address of the
resulting binary data. By default this is the minimum address
in the binary.

`padding` is the word value of the padding between
non-adjacent segments. Give as a bytes object of length 1 when
the word size is 8 bits, length 2 when the word size is 16
bits, and so on. By default the padding is `b'\xff' *
word_size_bytes`.

```pycon
>>> binfile.as_array()
'0x21, 0x46, 0x01, 0x36, 0x01, 0x21, 0x47, 0x01, 0x36, 0x00, 0x7e,
 0xfe, 0x09, 0xd2, 0x19, 0x01, 0x21, 0x46, 0x01, 0x7e, 0x17, 0xc2,
 0x00, 0x01, 0xff, 0x5f, 0x16, 0x00, 0x21, 0x48, 0x01, 0x19, 0x19,
 0x4e, 0x79, 0x23, 0x46, 0x23, 0x96, 0x57, 0x78, 0x23, 0x9e, 0xda,
 0x3f, 0x01, 0xb2, 0xca, 0x3f, 0x01, 0x56, 0x70, 0x2b, 0x5e, 0x71,
 0x2b, 0x72, 0x2b, 0x73, 0x21, 0x46, 0x01, 0x34, 0x21'
```

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.fill
documentation:
Fill empty space between segments.

`value` is the value which is used to fill the empty space. By
default the value is `b'\xff' * word_size_bytes`.

`max_words` is the maximum number of words to fill between the
segments. Empty space which larger than this is not
touched. If `None`, all empty space is filled.

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.exclude
documentation:
Exclude given range and keep the rest.

`minimum_address` is the first word address to exclude
(including).

`maximum_address` is the last word address to exclude
(excluding).

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.crop
documentation:
Keep given range and discard the rest.

`minimum_address` is the first word address to keep
(including).

`maximum_address` is the last word address to keep
(excluding).

methods.name: bincopy.BinFile.info
documentation:
Return a string of human readable information about the binary
file.

```python
>>> print(binfile.info())
Data ranges:

    0x00000100 - 0x00000140 (64 bytes)
```

A binary file.

`filenames` may be a single file or a list of files. Each file is
opened and its data added, given that the format is Motorola
S-Records, Intel HEX or TI-TXT.

Set `overwrite` to `True` to allow already added data to be
overwritten.

`word_size_bits` is the number of bits per word.

`header_encoding` is the encoding used to encode and decode the
file header (if any). Give as `None` to disable encoding,
leaving the header as an untouched bytes object.
